title: The Value of Slow Travel
sentences: Whereas most tourists rush between famous sites, I prefer to travel slowly.
Spending an entire week in one small town might sound dull to some.
In my experience, however, it offers a far richer understanding of a place.
By staying longer, you get to know local shopkeepers and neighbours.
Although this approach takes more time, it rarely costs more money.
Some travellers worry they will miss out on major attractions.
Personally, I would rather have a few genuine experiences than a long checklist.
Having tried both styles of travel, I now clearly prefer the slower one.
It allows unexpected moments to unfold without a rigid schedule getting in the way.
What seems inefficient at first often turns out to be deeply rewarding.
